entry.title: SAP B1 HANA approval using queries
entry.content:
Can anyone please guide me through approval stages in sap b1.I am using HANA dB.Currently I am implementing approval for purchase request.And I have udf field included in header table with valid values:PO and Good Issue.Currently if we link one approval stages with template(with 4 authorizers),it defaults to parallel approval where the approval document is sent to every users included in authorizers.So to simulate sequential authorization in such a way that only after first user approve,it will be forwarded to second approver based on value in Udf I have mentioned already,I have created stages each involving one approver and linked them all to one approval template.It should be like if value is PO,the approval should activate but if it is Good Issue,no approval needed.Here is my HANA approval query SELECT DISTINCT 'TRUE'

FROM DUMMY T0

WHERE $[OPRQ.U_PR_TYPE] = 'PO'.

Now the problem is it works for first approver ,but subsequent approval for approver 2,3 and 4 does not work.Can anyone help me how to get this right

entry.title: Re: EDI: Partner profile inbound not available Message no. E0337
entry.content:
Although this thread is medieval aI am posting a possible solution (cause I ran into the same issue and had to raise a case at SAP to get this resolved). I just paste the solution below:  ".....The problem is described in note 1776471 under section:

"One other very special case can be where key fields in the control record of the idoc actually matches the information in table EDP21. But the idoc still receives a status of 56. For the fields RCVPFC and SNDPFC we use in all dialogs the conversion form EDIPV (because the used domain is EDI_PARVW). On the database we don't save the value depending on the used language as there is a conversion exit behind that field so that you do not see the value that is stored on the database, but rather a language dependent one. On all dynpros you can insert 'VN' and before saving on the database this value will be converted to 'LF'. If you look at the entries then you see 'VN', but on the database you can find only 'LF'. If you send IDocs, then the fields RCVPFC and SNDPFC have to be filled with 'LF' instead of 'VN' to account for this. "....."

best regards, André

entry.title: Re: Functional Area Hierarchy in Group Financial Statemen...
entry.content:
Hi Tomi.

The Best Practise way (and best way in my opinion) is to use Reporting rules to prepare your P&L by Function.

This way you can combine FS items with Subitems and define the Functional P&L that way.

The resulting P&L can be presentes in both Group Data Analysis reports and Review Booklet by selecting the appropriate Reporting Hierarchy.

Br.

Jan

entry.title: Re: Sap Public Cloud - auto population of the account ass...
entry.content:
Hello dear user,

And thank you for asking your question in the SAP Community blog.

There’s a BADI called MM_PUR_S4_PO_MODIFY_ACCOUNT that you can implement.

Here’s some elements to be considered and to take into account :

 

Use

This Business Add-In (BAdI) is used in the Purchasing (MM-PUR-PO) component.

You can use this BAdI to make changes to fields on account assignment level.

Using the BAdI you can change the following fields:

Fieldname on the User Interface Technical Name

G/L Account Number GLACCOUNT

Cost Center COSTCENTER
Profit Center PROFITCENTER

Network Number for Account Assignment PROJECTNETWORK

Work Breakdown Structure Element (WBS Element) WBSELEMENTINTERNALID

Main Asset Number MASTERFIXEDASSET

Sales and Distribution Document Number SALESORDER

Sales Document Item SALESORDERITEM

Unloading Point UNLOADINGPOINT

Controlling Area CONTROLLINGAREA

Functional Area FUNCTIONALAREA

Service Document Type SERVICEDOCUMENTTYPE

Service Document ID SERVICEDOCUMENTID

Service Document Item ID SERVICEDOCUMENTITEMID

Goods Recipient GOODSRECIPIENTNAME

Order Number ORDERID

Note: Use the technical name of the fields for the BAdI implementation. Ensure that you do not delete standard fields unless you want to delete them. Note that SAP may add new fields and do not use statements that change the overall structure.

 

The following fields are not changeable:

Fieldname on User Interface Technical Name

Purchasing Document Number PURCHASEORDER

Item number of Purchasing Document PURCHASEORDERITEM

Sequential Number of Account Assignment ACCOUNTASSIGNMENTNUMBER

Using the BAdI, you can raise messages by filling the changing parameter table MESSAGES.

 

Standard Settings

  • The BAdI is called for each account assignment line separately.
  • The BAdI is active in the standard system supplied.

 

Activities

For information about implementing BAdIs as part of the Enhancement Concept, see SAP Library for SAP NetWeaver under BAdIs - Embedding in the Enhancement Framework.

 

Example 1

Modify account assignment goodsrecipientname:

READ TABLE purorderaccount_table INDEX 1 ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<ls_accountassignment>).

READ TABLE purchaseorderitem_table INDEX 1 ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<ls_pur_item>).

IF <ls_accountassignment> IS ASSIGNED AND <ls_pur_item> IS ASSIGNED.

IF <ls_pur_item>-accountassignmentcategory = 'K'.

purchaseorderaccountchange-goodsrecipientname = 'Testuser'.

ENDIF.

ENDIF.

 

Example 2

Check if accountassignmentcategory field is initial and then raise an error message. Here a non-translatable message is raised:

IF <ls_pur_item>-accountassignmentcategory = ''.

ls_message-messagetype = 'E'.

ls_message-messagevariable1 = 'The accountassignementcategory
cannot be initial'.

ls_message-messageid = 'MEPO'.

ls_message-messagenumber = '250'.

APPEND ls_message TO messages.
ENDIF.

 

I hope this will be helpful for you.

Best regards,

Jeremy

entry.title: Webinar: Configure-to-Order with ETO Special Requests across CPQ, Cideon Conify, CAD, S/4HANA AVC
entry.content:
While we have outlined the quote-to-cash end to end flow for configure-to-order across SAP CPQ, SAP Commerce Cloud, 3rd party applications, and SAP S/4HANA with Advanced Variant Configuration, in past sessions, in this webinar we would like to show you the end-to-end process for CTO+, including a detailed demo.

Please sign up here to join the webinar live, or review the recording later! 

The end-to-end Configure-to-Order Plus (CTO+) process enhances the traditional quote-to-cash cycle by accommodating both configured products and customer-specific engineering requests. It begins when a customer places an order for a configurable product that includes an additional Engineer-to-Order (ETO) requirement. SAP CPQ is used to configure the product, calculate pricing, costing, and margins, ensuring the offer is accurate and competitive. This front-end process may be supported by SAP Commerce Cloud or integrated third-party applications.

Once the configuration is completed, Cideon Conify automatically generates a CAD drawing of the configured product. This drawing is accessible directly from SAP CPQ and can be used for factory automation and virtual simulations in platforms like Nvidia Omniverse. If the customer requires additional engineering (ETO), the CAD drawing can be extended using SAP Engineering Control Center (ECTR) and the CAD system. Standard assemblies from the configured bill of materials (BOM) are reused, and new order-specific assemblies are created within the CAD environment.

After the engineering changes are finalized, the updated CAD data is transferred to SAP S/4HANA. This data forms an order-specific BOM, which is then released to production. The manufacturing process proceeds based on this customized BOM, ensuring that the final product meets the unique specifications requested by the customer. This integrated approach streamlines the handling of both standard and special orders within an integrated process and a high degree of automation.

entry.title: Chart_1.getDataSource().getDimensionFilters() not returning any values
entry.content:
Hi Folks,

I got a heat map chart. Attached to chart is an input control for dimension profitcenter - multiple values.
Input control is only for that heatmap.

I need to determine if a filter is set on the profitcenter dimension (before executing another script).

I planned to use getDimensionFilters() with following code

var value = Chart_16.getDataSource().getDimensionFilters("Profitcenter")[0];

switch (value.type) {
    case FilterValueType.Single:
        var singleValue = cast(Type.SingleFilterValue, value);
        console.log(singleValue.value); // can access the 'value' property now
        break;
    case FilterValueType.Multiple:
        var multipleValue = cast(Type.MultipleFilterValue, value);
        console.log(multipleValue.values); // can access the 'values' property now
        break;
    case FilterValueType.Range:
        var rangeValue = cast(Type.RangeFilterValue, value);
        console.log(rangeValue.from); // can access the 'from' property now
        console.log(rangeValue.to);   // can access the 'to' property now
        // further range properties: 'less', 'lessOrEqual', 'greater', 'greaterOrEqual'
        break;
    default:
        break;
}

However, the array is always empty. No matter if dimension filter via the input control is set or not.

Is this function not working for charts? Or is there a general problem when the dimension is filtered via input control?

Thanks for all insights in advance!

Best regards
Axel

entry.title: Re: Handling Bank Charges During Incoming Payments in SAP...
entry.content:
Hi ru5,

that is a common challenge and their are at least two approaches to solve it.

  1. Forward amount of bank charge from electronic account statement to payment lot
    If you create the payment lot based on electronic account statement, add the charge amount to the payment amount in payment lot. In your example add 10,- to 100,- and post payment lot with 110,-. You have to take care that the difference is still posted on an expense account. Check exits 0950 following and follwing ones.
  2. Use tolerance group
    Set up tolerance group to 10,- and create a clearing variant used for clearing type incoming payments to make a full clearing considering the tolerance group. 

Kind Regards,

Tobias

entry.title: Where can I learn how to use SAP IBP Time Series Based Constrained Supply Planning with Optimize ?
entry.content:
I recommend to access our Mastering the supply optimizer in SAP IBP for Time-Series Based Constrained Supply Planning learning journey.
With the completion of this learning journey, you will be prepared for the certification C_S4CS. 

Learning Objectives
In this comprehensive course you will gain a solid understanding of the SAP IBP Time Series Based Supply Planning business processes using Optimizer, and their associated configuration.
DO NOT attempt to take this course without first completing the recommended courses for SAP IBP for Supply Chain Overview (IBP1010, IBP1020, IBP2200, IBP2400, IBP2500, IBP2600, IBP2700 & IBP2800), for Discovering the Main Configuration for SAP IBP (IBP2300) AND for SAP IBP Platform Features and Time Series Based Heuristics Planning (IBP3210 & IBP3220). If you attempt to take IBP3710 without first completing the mandatory prerequisite courses, you will not achieve the maximum benefits, and some concepts may be difficult to comprehend.​

Learning objectives
This course will prepare you to: 

  • ​Outline the features of SAP IBP Time Series Based Constrained Supply Planning with Optimizer  
  • Describe in detail: Modeling, Key Figures, and Parameters  
  • Utilize Operators and Optimizers 

Prerequisites
Essential: 

  • ​IBP1010 - Exploring Planning Processes in SAP IBP
  • IBP1020 - Exploring Tools & Features in SAP IBP
  • IBP2200 – Discovering SAP IBP for S&OP
  • IBP2300 –Discovering the Main Configuration for SAP IBP
  • IBP2400 - Discovering SAP IBP for Inventory Planning and Optimization
  • IBP2500 - Discovering SAP Supply Chain Control Tower  

Please post you question related to the digital learning Journey in the Q&A area. 
Our SAP Learning Experts will get back to you as soon as possible! We are here to support you. 

I appreciate your feedback and we will make sure to continue sharing interesting topics.

Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: Release Navigator for SAP BTP - What's New in Q2 2025
entry.content:
The Release Navigator for SAP BTP continues to evolve and expand, offering our customers improved resources and streamlined access to product updates. As we step into Q2 2025, we are excited to announce the addition of 11 new product pages. Read more about the details in this blog post.

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

Collaborating closely with SAP BTP experts, we've expanded the release coverage by introducing 11 new product pages. These pages compile links to all available release information, such as schedules, pre- and post-release communications, blogs, and more:

  • SAP Audit Log Service
  • SAP Authorization and Trust Management Service
  • SAP BTP, Cloud Foundry environment
  • SAP Connectivity Service
  • SAP Credential Store
  • SAP Custom Domain Service
  • SAP Destination Service
  • SAP Integration Suite, Advanced Event Mesh
  • SAP Malware Scanning Service
  • SAP Private Link Service
  • SAP Secure Login Service for SAP GUI

You will notice that several of these pages share certain resources which are relevant across SAP BTP services that are relating to security.

Here's a glimpse at our new additions:

 

1. SAP Audit Log Service

This page contains specific resources for SAP Audit Log service as well as key release resources for SAP BTP security services, which also partially cover SAP Audit Log service among others. Here’s a list of what you can find on this page:

  • SAP Audit Log service
    • Release Schedule in SAP Note
    • What’s New Viewer: Release Notes
    • Documentation: SAP Help Portal
    • SAP Discovery Center
  • SAP BTP Security services
    • SAP Roadmap Explorer
    • SAP Community Page & Tag
    • Documentation: SAP Help Portal
    • SAP Security Products and Solutions Newsletter
    • Customer Influence

Check it out.

 

2. SAP Authorization and Trust Management Service

This page contains specific resources for SAP Audit Log service as well as key release resources for SAP BTP security services, which also partially cover SAP Audit Log service among others. Here’s a list of what you can find on this page:

entry.title: Language set in BO OLAP connection doesn't work with DATASPHERE
entry.content:
Hi all,
in our organization we detect this problem:
1- we migrated a BO Webi from BW source (Bex) to  DATASPHERE source (Analytical Model)
2- the connection to Datasphere was set in  OLAP connection section and it works
2- in the OLAP connection the flag on "Save language" was inserted

4- the authentication in SSO (there are no other choices because SSO was set in DATASPHERE)
5- the webi report returns the data in the browser language (English) and doesn't consider the language set in the OLAP connection ( in our case Italian).
This also happens when scheduling the webi report or scheduling the publication  that has the webi report as its source.

The BO version is :
SAP BusinessObjects BI Platform 4.3 Support Pack 4 Patch 8
Version: 14.3.4.5099

Any idea to solve it?

Anna M

Thanks for your assistance...

entry.title: Re: Unable to Populate SUN Field (Service User Number) in...
entry.content:
See help.sap.com documentation Configuring Payment Medium Formats 

See as referenced in same 

  1. To be able to fill out each field in the GB_BACS format, you can make settings in the following configuration activities:

    • Define User Number at Bank in Norway, United Kingdom, and Ireland (ID: 103132)

      In this configuration step, you define a user number at the bank per company code, country, and bank number.

    • Define Bank Transaction Code for United Kingdom (ID: 103131)

      In this configuration step, you define the bank transaction code for the customer/vendor and the bank transaction code for the house bank per country/region, payment method, and bank number.

    • Enter Name of Building Society (ID: 103133)

      In this configuration step, you enter the name of the building society for each bank or bank account.


      See 

      payment medium format GB_BACS

      This payment format defined by BACS is used for domestic direct debit and Bacs direct credit.

      Payments by BACS - SAP Documentation

entry.title: Open Connectors via APIM:
entry.content:
1. Create an API Provider for Open connectors Service Now instance using Type “Open Connectors”

 

  1. Create a proxy using API provider created in step1

No policies created on API Proxy, as we are going to pass Authorization on header in postman

  1. Create a product & add API proxy to the product

  1. Create an application in developer HUB with product created in step3 & fetch APP key & Secret for authentication purpose.

 

Testing:

  1. Use the PROXY endpoint along with the resource path to call. Use the API key & secret as authentication. Use the authorization in header for Open connectors

  1. Get incidents from Open connector Instance of Service Now:

Try to fetch the same from Postman as well using the suffix part of the URL to call incidents resource (/incidents)

Regards,

Rohini Potham

entry.title: Re: Weighted interview questions
entry.content:
Hi,

Yes,You're correct that SAP SuccessFactors Recruiting doesn't natively support weighted scoring in the way your current system does.

However, there are alternative approaches and configurations within SuccessFactors that can help you achieve a similar outcome.

  1. Explore Pre-Screening Questions: Implement weighted pre-screening questions to capture essential qualifications early in the hiring process.
  2. Define Competencies: Clearly define competencies for each role and assign appropriate weights to reflect their importance.
  3. Customize Rating Scales: Develop custom rating scales that align with your organization's evaluation criteria.
  4. Utilize AI Insights: Leverage AI-assisted feedback tools to gain deeper insights into candidate evaluations.
  5. Consider Manual Calculations: Use external tools for complex calculations and reporting needs.

Hope this helps!! Please let me know if you need assistance on configuring these features within SF or developing custom reports.

Thanks,

kr,

karthick.

entry.title: Customer Influence and Adoption for SAP FS-CD
entry.content:
Continuous Influence Sessions

Ideas and development requests can be submitted directly to SAP via various programs. SAP offers, for example, Continuous Influence Sessions.

User Groups

There are also other channels for influencing development, for example, through various user groups such as DSAG or ASUG. There are also product-specific user groups such as the FS-CD DACH User Group for German-speaking FS-CD users.

Creating a request via Continuous Influence Sessions

In this article, I will focus on Continuous Influence Sessions. To participate, you must register at Customer Influencer. The introductory pages contain helpful links to information on how these Continuous Influence Sessions work.

 

Unfortunately, the challenge here is that there is no dedicated session specifically for FS-CD. As a customer, you can only contribute your ideas to existing sessions.

As with many other SAP resources, it helps to know how SAP Collections and Disbursements for Insurance is used in SAP solutions. Firstly, it's based on the industry-neutral SAP Contract Accounts Receivable and Payable and is part of SAP Billing and Revenue Innovation Management . This means you can also search for sessions on these two solutions to submit ideas for FS-CD

Using the search function, you can then find a current session for BRIM and submit your cross-industry development requests there.

 

After submitting a development request, other users can vote for it.

 

It's a good idea to coordinate within the user groups to get as many votes as possible. Once a certain number of votes are received, the development request is reviewed by the respective product manager. This isn't a guarantee of implementation, but it is the first step toward a new feature.

entry.title: Re: Country version Spain SEPA file
entry.content:
Check the payment medium format used for payment.

See apps Manage Automatic Payments and Manage Payment medium 

 

See help.sap.com documentation 
https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/a624a4a6d1d8473eb95fb42660127cfe/d6af5407b276477f9698f816e684ae27.html?locale=en-US&q=ES_CGI_XML_CT_V9

 

If payment medium format is used ES_CGI_XML_CT_V9. See version of pain referenced below. 

This payment format is a new version of the CGI format, it is version pain.001.001.09. It is available in addition to version pain.001.001.03.

entry.title: My Journey to Becoming SAP Certified: From Missed Opportunity to TS410 Success
entry.content:
I don't know if this should be a blog or just a comment under the post about Free SAP Certification and Practice Systems for Students — but I felt compelled to share my story.

Late 2023 was a turning point for me. I had just switched careers into the SAP ecosystem and was fortunate to secure a SAP Application Support role in early 2024. By August, I had progressed to an Application Manager position. While the growth was exciting, it came with real-world challenges — one of them being the missed opportunity to use the free certification code I received from the Digital Skills Initiative. With work pressures and the need to build foundational SAP knowledge, I couldn't prepare in time, and the code eventually expired. Despite my efforts, I couldn’t redeem it again.

Still, I didn’t give up. My interest in SAP had grown into a passion. I later enrolled in a postgraduate ERP (SAP) Functional Consultant programme at one of SAP’s University Partners, which also qualifies me for the new Digital Skills Initiative (Free SAP Certification and Practice Systems for Students) and access to practice systems. I saw it as a second chance — and I grabbed it with both hands.

I activated my student subscription through the initiative and committed to my studies with renewed focus. I’m beyond thrilled (and honestly, emotional) to share that I passed my SAP Certified Application Associate – Business Process Integration with SAP S/4HANA (TS410) certification exam on Sunday, May 11th — Mother's Day. It was a significant day for me both personally and professionally. That day now holds even more meaning for me — and the cover photo I’ve used here was actually a Mother's Day gift, symbolizing celebration, growth, and gratitude.

To those just beginning their journey or feeling discouraged after a setback — I want you to know: don’t give up. Your path may not be linear or perfect, but every experience builds you. These SAP resources, from learning journeys to practice systems and community support, are truly invaluable. Make the most of them. Keep learning, keep growing, and show up for yourself every step of the way.

To the SAP Learning team and everyone behind the @Digital Skills Initiative — thank you for making global upskilling a reality. To fellow learners and community members: you’ve got this. 💪🌟

entry.title: Fixed Assets Migration - Asset Value Date and Depreciation calculation Issue
entry.content:
Dear SAP Experts,

As part of our fixed assets migration, we are experiencing an issue with the depreciation calculation.

The assets in question are being migrated during the fiscal year, and the Legacy Data Transfer Settings have been configured in the application “Make Company Code Settings – Asset Accounting Specific”, with a Transfer Date of 30/04/2025.

However, after migrating the assets, we noticed that the depreciation calculation started from 01/01/2025 instead of from period 04/2025.

Upon reviewing the assets post-migration, we found that the Asset Value Date was set to 01/01/2025, even though we did not populate this field in the migration template. We believe that this field is the root cause of the issue.

Could you please clarify how this field is derived?

For your information, we filled in the following three tabs in the “Fixed Assets – Postings” migration object: Posting Key ; Cumulative Values ;Transaction Headers.

We also noticed that the Asset Value Date field exists in the Transaction Items tab, which we did not initially complete. We did tests by filling in this tab, but encountered errors due to the Asset Transaction Type 970 (Asset Data Transfer) we used.

Is there another asset transaction type that should be used for mid-year data migrations ? Please suggest an alternative solution if possible.

Thank you in advance for your help.

entry.title: Product Inspiration Series - Experience a New Era of SAP CX (wave #1)
entry.content:
Hi everyone, I'm excited to share our latest deep dive videos as part of the SAP Product Inspiration Series 2025 focusing on innovations across SAP CX and Consumer Industries. 

Wave #1 (April 2025):

1. Predictive Planning and Inventory Orchestration from SAP

Vinayak Kini from the Retail Product Team at SAP provides an overview on Predictive Planning and Inventory Orchestration. Watch this product inspiration video to learn more about this new end-to-end cloud solution covering Order and Demand Scheduling, Unified Demand Forecasting and Predictive Replenishment as well as our major innovations as part of the Intelligent CX portfolio from SAP.

The key highlights shared in this video are:
00:00 - Introduction
01:12 - Why Predictive Planning & Inventory Orchestration from SAP?
03:02 - Where can SAP’s solution help transform a Retailer’s operations?
05:29 - How can retailers implement Predictive Planning & Inventory Orchestration from SAP?
06:46 - Start of the Demo
06:58 - Demo: SAP Order and Delivery Scheduling
09:02 - Demo: Unified Demand Forecast in SAP Customer Activity Repository
11:15 - Demo: Review Order Plan Items in SAP Customer Activity Repository
13:58 - Demo: SAP Predictive Replenishment
18:54 - Summary: Intelligent CX from SAP

 

2. Extensibility of SAP Sales and Service Cloud V2 using No Code / Low Code Tools

Join an in-depth session between Sophie Crepin from SAP Customer Success and Vinay Prasad from SAP Product Engineering regarding no-code low-code extensibility of SAP Sales and Service Cloud Version 2. Watch this product inspiration video to learn how easy it is to adapt and extend your SAP cloud solution and hear about major innovations as part of the Intelligent CX portfolio from SAP.

The key highlights shared in this video are:
00:00 - Introduction
03:26 - Start of the Demo
03:37 - Demo: Defining Extension Fields and Validation Logic
07:39 - Demo: Building Lightweight Extensions using SAP Build Apps
12:35 - Demo: Creating Code List Restrictions
13:53 - Demo: Using Dynamic Page Layouts
15:41 - Demo: Embedding Custom Services
17:22 - Summary: Intelligent CX from SAP

 

3. Sinch Omnichannel Customer Engagement for SAP CX

Venky Naravulu from the Sinch Product Team provides an overview on Sinch’s Omnichannel Messaging, Chatlayer and Contact Pro and how it integrates with SAP CX. Watch this product inspiration video to learn how AI-powered omnichannel marketing interactions and customer service can be delivered using Sinch’s Omnichannel Messaging, Chatlayer and Contact Pro along with SAP Emarsys and SAP Service Cloud Version 2.

The key highlights shared in this video are:
00:00 - Introduction
00:40 - Why Sinch and SAP CX?
01:59 - How can customers benefit from our joint solution?
03:38 - How is AI used in Sinch and along the SAP CX integration?
06:06 - Start of the Demo
07:05 - Demo: Sinch Template Creation for WhatsApp
10:39 - Demo: Sinch and SAP Emarsys Campaign Automation
14:06 - Demo: Sinch Contact Pro and WhatsApp integration using GenAI
19:53 - Demo: Sinch Contact Pro and SAP Service Cloud Version 2 Integration
25:00 - Summary: Intelligent CX from SAP

 

4. Open Payment Framework for SAP Commerce Cloud in Action

Wendy Wang from the Commerce Product Team at SAP provides an overview on Open Payment Framework (OPF). Watch this product inspiration video to learn how OPF for SAP Commerce Cloud providing a plug-and-play framework to ease integration complexity and offer composable solutions, enabling retailers to quickly and conveniently expand payment options across various markets.

The key highlights shared in this video are:
00:00 - Introduction
00:43 - Why Open Payment Framework (OPF) for SAP Commerce Cloud?
01:26 - Which scenarios does SAP’s OPF offer?
02:31 - How can customers adopt SAP’s OPF?
03:57 - Start of the Demo
04:08 - Demo: SAP Commerce Cloud storefront with check-out payment options
06:13 - Demo: SAP Open Payment Framework - Site Assignment
07:35 - Demo: SAP Open Payment Framework - Refund Configuration
09:54 - Demo: SAP Open Payment Framework - Authorization Configuration
11:56 - Demo: SAP Open Payment Framework - Payment Transactions
13:35 - Demo: SAP Open Payment Framework - Manual Refund
14:50 - Demo: SAP Open Payment Framework - Logs
16:21 - Demo: SAP Open Payment Framework - Compliance

 

5. Open Search Service for SAP Commerce Cloud in Action

Etienne Guilhaume from the Commerce Product Team at SAP provides an overview on the new Search Service. Watch this product inspiration video to learn how use the Search Service along with Intelligent Selling Services in SAP Commerce Cloud to drive conversion and accelerate growth.

The key highlights shared in this video are:
00:00 - Introduction
00:27 - Why the Search Service for SAP Commerce Cloud?
01:56 - Where can the Search Service be very beneficial?
02:32 - How can SAP customers adopt the Search Service?
03:28 - Start of the demo
03:50 - Demo: Product search in SAP Commerce Cloud storefront
04:48 - Demo: Related products on Product Detail Page

entry.title: Bridging Real-World Safety Practices with Digital Compliance: The Role of SAP EHS
entry.content:
Introduction

Environment, Health, and Safety (EHS) is not just a department—it's a critical function woven in day-to-day operations of companies across industries such as manufacturing, oil & gas, chemicals, pharmaceuticals, and energy. Whether it's managing chemical hazards, ensuring worker safety, or adhering to environmental regulations, every organization has developed its own set of EHS practices.

But how do these manual or semi-digital processes translate into enterprise-wide, standardized, and auditable systems? That’s where SAP EHS (Environment, Health, and Safety Management) steps in—aligning industry practices with regulatory needs through structured digital workflows.

How Industries Handle EHS Today

Many organizations implement EHS practices in the form of:

  • Safety checklists and paper-based inspections
  • Spreadsheet tracking of chemical inventory or incidents
  • Email-based reporting and approvals
  • Periodic compliance audits using manual templates
  • Localized document storage of Safety Data Sheets (SDS)

While these methods may work in warehouse or for small teams, they often result in:

  • Delayed incident responses
  • Gaps in regulatory compliance
  • Inconsistent data reporting
  • Lack of traceability and audit readiness

What is SAP EHS?

SAP EHS is an integrated component of SAP S/4HANA that digitizes and standardizes all aspects of EHS management. It allows businesses to proactively manage risks, ensure compliance, and support employee health and safety with greater control and visibility.

The solution covers:

  • Product Safety & Compliance
  • Occupational Health Management
  • Incident Management & Risk Assessment
  • Dangerous Goods Handling
  • Substance Volume Tracking (SVT)
  • Environmental Management & Emissions Tracking

By centralizing EHS processes, SAP ensures that what used to be fragmented, and manual becomes automated, standardized, and globally auditable.

Comparing Industry Practice vs. SAP EHS Implementation

Aspect

Traditional Industry Practice

With SAP EHS

Incident Reporting

Manual, delayed escalation

Real-time capture and tracking

SDS Management

Stored locally or emailed

Centralized, version-controlled documentation

Risk Assessment

Excel or Word templates

Structured, workflow-driven risk evaluations

Regulatory Compliance

Regional and siloed efforts

Global compliance with integrated regulations

Health Surveillance

Paper-based records or local systems

Complete employee health history and tracking

Audit Readiness

Manual report compilation

Dashboards, KPIs, and audit trails in one place

 

Why SAP EHS Matters Today

With rising regulatory pressure, ESG reporting requirements, and a growing emphasis on worker wellbeing, businesses can no longer afford to treat EHS as a back-office function. SAP EHS not only ensures legal compliance but also builds a culture of proactive safety, risk awareness, and environmental stewardship.

Key benefits include:

  • Improved operational transparency
  • Better decision-making with real-time data
  • Global standardization of safety practices
  • Alignment with sustainability and ESG goals

Why Choose SAP EHS Over Third-Party EHS Tools?

Many companies evaluate third-party EHS platforms that promise modern user interfaces or industry-specific features. However, SAP EHS stands out for organizations already running on SAP ERP or S/4HANA due to several strategic, functional, and usability advantages:

1. Native Integration with SAP ERP/S/4HANA

  • No need for middleware or complex interfaces.
  • EHS processes are tightly linked to materials management, HR master data, logistics, production, and procurement.
  • Seamless data flow improves accuracy and efficiency across business functions.

2. Unified User Experience with SAP Fiori

  • SAP EHS leverages the modern SAP Fiori UI, which is responsive, role-based, and mobile-ready.
  • Safety professionals, plant users, and managers can access the system on tablets, phones, or desktops with a consistent interface.
  • Personalized dashboards and quick actions reduce user training time.

3. Enhanced Security and Compliance

  • SAP’s robust role-based authorization ensures data protection and regulatory compliance.
  • All activities are logged and auditable—crucial for internal audits, ISO certifications, or legal inquiries.

4. Real-Time Analytics and Reporting

  • Leverage SAP Embedded Analytics or SAP Analytics Cloud to get real-time insights into incidents, compliance KPIs, risk levels, and more.
  • Drill-down capabilities help management take faster, data-driven decisions.

5. Long-Term Scalability and Support

  • SAP EHS evolves with your business and regulatory landscape—especially with the shift to ESG and carbon accounting.
  • Supported by SAP’s roadmap and continuous innovation, unlike niche third-party tools which may face support or scalability limitations.

6. Lower TCO (Total Cost of Ownership)

  • Organizations with an existing SAP footprint benefit from license reusability, shared infrastructure, and a single support ecosystem.
  • No need for separate integrations or duplicate master data maintenance.

Real-World Example

Let’s say a manufacturing company uses a third-party tool to manage incidents, but their procurement and production data lies in SAP. This often leads to:

  • Delays in root-cause analysis (data mismatch),
  • Repetitive data entry (duplication),
  • Gaps in traceability (no direct link to materials or equipment).

In SAP EHS, these are handled in the same system—linking incidents to hazardous substances, material movements, work centers, and even employees.

Conclusion

Every company already practices some form of EHS. The real transformation happens when those practices are digitized, standardized, and scaled using enterprise-grade solutions like SAP EHS. It's not just about compliance—it's about building a safer, more sustainable, and future-ready organization.

If you're still relying on manual or semi-digital EHS processes, it may be time to rethink how technology like SAP can elevate your safety culture.

Regards,

Ramnarayana Valluri

Emma Le Goff, Product Manager at Akeneo, provides an overview on Akeneo's Product Cloud and how it integrates with SAP CX. entry.title: Re: Securing Your Data with Data Access Controls in Datasphere
entry.content: I'm looking into DataAccessControl and can only find that this can be maintained manually. Users and Role/Scooped role assignment can be automatically created/provisioned via attributes in SAML ticket. But I can't find anything that indicates DataAccessControl also to be updated automatically from SAML ticket. If I have user attributes, role assignment and organizationsal level information from IdP (included in SAML ticket), isn't it possible to update also DataAccessControl in DataSphere? Strange if this is a separate manual process when the other things can be automized.

entry.title: Are you ready to use Concur Expense Mobile on Android?
entry.content:
I recommend to access our Getting Started with Concur Expense Mobile Android for Users learning journey.
With the completion of this learning journey, you will be prepared for the certification C_S4CS. 

Learning Objectives
This learning journey with demos and practice simulations will introduce business users to the basics of Concur Expense (Mobile Android) such as profile features, creating a new expense report, the expense report approval process and roles, as well as additional expense report features needed to be successful in submitting their expense reports.

Learning objectives

  • Explain an overview of SAP Concur and how to do basic tasks (signing in, profile, home page).
  • Set Up Your Concur Expense Profile.
  • Create a New Expense Report and basic expense line items.
  • Explain What Happens Once You Submit your Expense Report.
  • Set up Additional Expense Report Features.

Please post you question related to the digital learning Journey in the Q&A area. 

Our SAP Learning Experts will get back to you as soon as possible! We are here to support you. 

I appreciate your feedback and we will make sure to continue sharing interesting topics.

Kind regards
Margit
